writings.title: Mr. Berry Goes Black
writings.summary: Excited to create a few cocktail recipes for El Silencio Mezcal. The first one is a take on one of my all-time favorites Ready Fire Aim from Employee Only NYC, using blackberry with bay leaf.

writings.title: Corretto
writings.summary: Inspired by a low ABV drink from Death and Co. Denver, a delicious corretto made with Amaro Montenegro, espresso, coconut milk, and pebble ice.

writings.title: Fuego de Caña
writings.summary: Celebrating the global Fair Trade movement with Flor de Caña — a Mai Tai riff pairing peanut-washed rum with banana and Strega, topped with a flaming marshmallow inspired by the active volcano at the distillery's base in Nicaragua.

writings.title: Where To Drink Cocktails In Singapore
writings.summary: It is my fourth visit to Singapore yet the first time I got to experience what the cocktail scene is like in Singapore. With the rapid growth of cocktail popularity over the last three years, Singapore is in the cocktail renaissance with five bars on the World's Top 50 Bar list.

writings.title: Apri-caught Showers from American Cococktail Co.
writings.summary: Last month, I had the pleasure to work with American Cocktail Co to design the upcoming April box: The Spicy Ginger Mule. This is a bourbon-based mule with the notes of apricot, just in time for the spring weather to enjoy at home.

writings.title: What's in Your Rum?
writings.summary: Copalli Distillery visit is the first trip where I learned first hand what sustainability means for the spirits industry.

writings.title: From Gin to Hand Sanitizer - Interview with Brad Plummer at Gin Farallon
writings.summary: An interview with Brad Plummer, founder of Gin Farallon, about pivoting from gin production to making hand sanitizer during the pandemic and donating to frontline workers.

writings.title: Mosuo — The Last Matriarchy in China
writings.summary: There are only 5-6 matriarchal societies left in the world. Last November, I spent seven days in Mosuo villages documenting seven families with a focus on the head of the household and their living environment.

writings.title: The Front Steps Project — Palo Alto Edition
writings.summary: May 7th to June 27th. Total photo sessions: 101. The total number of photos taken: >15000.

writings.title: Markets Around The World
writings.summary: Whenever I travel, I always look up the local markets and make sure I set aside time to visit. For me, it is the most authenticate experience in a foreign country, the heart & soul of the local pulse.

writings.title: Varanasi — The Good, The Bad and The Ugly
writings.summary: Varanasi was supposed to be the highlight of my trip, but my experience was very much crippled by sickness in the group due to Delhi Belly as well as the worrisome news of COVID-19.

writings.title: A Taste of Rajasthan
writings.summary: I have been obsessed with the idea of traveling to India especially Rajasthan: from desert scrubs, holy rivers, painted blue houses, to picturesque lakes.

writings.title: The Colors of Havana
writings.summary: Last November, I traveled to Cuba Havana for the first time, as part of the small photography group organized by the world-renowned photojournalist Peter Turnley.

writings.title: A Mother's Struggle
writings.summary: I hit a low point both physically and emotionally today, after being in the lockdown for six weeks. Nothing about my situation is unique or devastating, compared to the millions of Americans who have lost their jobs.

writings.title: Belle de Brillet
writings.summary: Belle de Brillet Pear Liqueur is an original blend of Brillet Cognac and the essence of Williams Pears — a luscious spirit with ripe tart pear flavor and the perfect balance of cognac.

writings.title: Prescription Julep
writings.summary: David Wondrich calls it "the tastiest Mint Julep recipe" he knows, here I am introducing you to an updated Prescription Julep on the #nationalcognacday, served in the new Norlan Vawe glass.

writings.title: Awakening the Dead
writings.summary: A refreshing tequila cocktail with Chamucos Tequila Diablo Blanco, Ancho Reyes verde, and a kick of dried chile peppers and hot sauce.

writings.title: Six Figures
writings.summary: An upgraded version of Six Figures, an over-elaborate brandy daisy made with French and American spirits to celebrate the 75th Anniversary of D-Day and support Cell Phones for Soldiers.

writings.title: Negroni Alla Fiama
writings.summary: Alla Fiama is where Negroni meets Bellini, by pulling back the classic's proportions to be low ABV this is a well-suited breakfast negroni.

writings.title: The Three Day Drink Guide for Oaxaca City
writings.summary: You are undeserving of Oaxaca City if you spend only three days there. But three-day is a great framework to be selective about your drink spots.

writings.title: The Sake Primer
writings.summary: I am excited to partner with Takara Sake to design a few cocktail recipes. A few weeks ago, I had the opportunity to visit Takara in Berkeley for a tasting and educational tour.

writings.title: Patrón — My First Agave Experience
writings.summary: This May, I was invited to visit Patrón through their agency M Booth in New York City. In this blog post, I will briefly touch on the history of Patron, my experience visiting Patron, its production as well as its contribution to the local community.

writings.title: Travel Guide: Through the Andean Mountains in Ecuador
writings.summary: This summer, I visited Ecuador for the first time. I didn't expect to be so spectacular in many ways: amazing landscape, diverse culture, and the most friendly people in Latin American I have ever met.

writings.title: Sonic Juicebox
writings.summary: A modified Juicebox Cobbler with a drier, lighter vermouth-ish blend featuring Cocchi Rosa, La Quintinye extra dry, Laubade Floc, and Braulio — letting summer fruits shine through.

writings.title: Catching Mr. Abagnale
writings.summary: A mezcal cocktail that takes the aromatic El Silencio Espadin for an international ride, with a vegetal undertone of dill, disguised in the tarty yuzu soda.

writings.title: A Midsummer Day's Apple
writings.summary: I remembered how much fruity apple notes was packed in that Aberlour 12! I built a new highball by combining Aberlour and Calvados, enhanced by Polite Amor bitters full of gingerbread and fall spices.

writings.title: The Very Thought of Suze
writings.summary: Suze makes every drink better! Inspired by a drink named Bring June Flowers on Punch that uses vodka and jasmine, a light and bitter sipper for the late summer with cucumber magic.

writings.title: The Tipsy Muse's Cobbler
writings.summary: The classic cobbler format is essentially a base spirit that is sweetened by some form of syrup and adorned with seasonal fruit. Here is the one I designed for Norlan Glass Vawe, using Old Elk bourbon with fresh berries and sage.

writings.title: Beet It
writings.summary: Beets and mezcal negroni. It is my first time to buy beets from the grocery, but I have wanted to use it in my cocktails for a while!

writings.title: The King's Old Fashioned
writings.summary: Inspired by The King's legendary peanut butter, banana and bacon sandwich, this peanut-infused bourbon Old Fashioned brings all three flavors together in one glass.

writings.title: Avoid Your Own Blind Spot
writings.summary: I met Dan Rasmuson, the up-and-coming young CTO of Labelbox through First Round Fast Track. During our first meeting, Dan's first question was 'How do I avoid my own blind spot?'

writings.title: The Tipple Road of Colorado
writings.summary: This Spring, I had the opportunity to take a road trip to Colorado. We went from Denver, Boulder, Rocky Mountain National Park, Breckenridge, Sand Dune National Park to Colorado Springs in 10 days.

writings.title: Leopold Bros. Distillery Visit
writings.summary: During the family vacation this April, I had the pleasure to visit Leopold Bros Distillery in Denver, a pioneer in the craft distillery movement known for its unique grain-to-glass approach and the only three chamber still in the world.

writings.title: Domenico Goes to Sardinia
writings.summary: An aperitif-style cocktail recipe designed for Disaronno to celebrate #DisaronnoDay, featuring Varnelli Dell'Erborista amaro with bittersweet layers of flavor rounded with amaretto.

writings.title: Bitter Sweet Symphony with Lucano
writings.summary: Hailing from the small town of Pisticci in Lucania, Italy, Amaro Lucano is made from a secret blend of 30-plus herbs and essential oils. A tiki-inspired cocktail that reflects its sweet and bitter taste with a minty profile.

writings.title: Winter Hibiscus Margarita
writings.summary: The flavor of hibiscus, lemony-tart, and berry-rich goes well with the soft and fruity Cazadores Reposado with a hint of woodiness. A winter cocktail recipe featuring Tequila Cazadores Reposado and homemade hibiscus syrup.

writings.title: Pellehaut 1994 Folle Blanche
writings.summary: Today, I would love to talk about Folle Blanche and this single vintage of 1994 Folle Blanche from Pellehaut.

writings.title: My Little Delord Family
writings.summary: Before I knew it, I am a proud owner of 5 different Armagnac Delord. A visit to the distillery, tasting notes on five expressions, and the story behind the Delord and Marie Duffau brands.

writings.title: Armagnac Lacqugy Colombard 2000
writings.summary: My first taste of Armagnac Lacguy was at Amanda's house where we tried Lacquy 17 yrs old blend. It turned out to be one of the favorites that evening.

writings.title: Paul John Edited
writings.summary: My very first bottle of Paul John is Edited, one of its flagship products. An Indian Single Malt Whisky from Goa with a honey nose, subtle peat, and a long finish of grassy spices and chocolate mint.

writings.title: Cognac Park Mizunara Oak Finish
writings.summary: Mizunara is a rare and expensive Japanese oak used to age premium spirits. Here I want to talk about the first ever Mizunara finished brandy from Cognac Park.

writings.title: Dewar's Double Double
writings.summary: I recently had the opportunity to work with Dewar's on their latest release: Double Double, and I enjoyed the experience of the three different sherry expressions.

writings.title: Smokie Sunshine
writings.summary: Many of us know Luxardo maraschino liqueur, but few know their new product Luxardo Bitter Bianco. A smoky, fruity summer cocktail inspired by Naked and Famous.

writings.title: Rum Sour with Fève
writings.summary: My second experiment with the 'magic bean' — a tonka bean-infused Smith & Cross Rum sour with toasted coconut syrup and Xocolatl Mole Bitters.

writings.title: Meriggiare
writings.summary: Summer is my favorite season: things are at a slower pace, not embarrassed to take a midday nap and it doesn't get dark til 9PM. This drink, created for Cinzano, is my way to express my yearning for summer months.

writings.title: Siam Sour
writings.summary: I infused some gin with fresh lemongrass and pink peppercorn to make a gin sour that highlights the flavors of Thailand.

writings.title: An Efflorescence of Spring
writings.summary: Embracing the week-long rain in Bay Area, and soon I will be rewarded with the blooming flowers of spring. With that in mind, I experimented with a new gin: Panda Gin from Belgium, with strong floral notes and lychee.

writings.title: Fitte et Laterrade 1979
writings.summary: During my week-long visit to the Armagnac region, one of the unique Armagnac houses we met is Armagnac Fitte et Laterrade, where Vincent Laterrade delivers vintage directly from the barrels with nothing else added.

writings.title: Penang Swizzle
writings.summary: After sharing cocktails on Instagram for almost two years, I finally learned about swizzled drinks and have my own swizzle stick, thanks to the awesome Swizzle Royale event organized by The House of ANGOSTURA.

writings.title: Croft-y Pear Julep
writings.summary: Excited to partner with Croft Port, one of the oldest original Port houses, to create an original cocktail recipe for this merry holiday season using Croft Reserve Tawny Port, Armagnac, and pear brandy.

writings.title: Where to Start with Armagnac
writings.summary: After nine months of exploring over 15 bottles, a guide to the first few Armagnac bottles to get started — from mixing to tasting.

writings.title: Exitus Wine in Cocktails
writings.summary: Mixing red wine with spirits is not always easy. Recently, I had the opportunity to develop a few recipes for Exitus Wine from O'Neil Vintners & Distillers, a bourbon barrel aged red wine that tiptoes on the boundary with spirits.

writings.title: Between the Wires: An interview with Sindre Sorhus
writings.summary: An interview with Sindre Sorhus, a prolific open source developer who lives in Thailand, discussing his journey from childhood Legos to maintaining over 1000 npm packages.

writings.title: Between the Wires: An interview with Karissa McKelvey
writings.summary: An interview with Karissa McKelvey, Director of Engineering at Dat Project, a distributed data sharing tool that packages your data and shares it over a distributed network.

writings.title: Pine Nuts Washed Old Fashioned
writings.summary: I made my pine nuts washed Old Fashioned again, but each time with improved techniques and subtle changes. This time, I chose Rye — half High West Rendezvous Rye and half Wild Turkey 101 Rye to get the right spice and heat.

writings.title: Iron Lady — Do It The French Way
writings.summary: I picked up this little cute book named 'Do It The French Way' — a wonderful history book about my favorite French apéritif. Here is a little drink I made as I was learning the French way to reserve time for myself, friends, bottles and conviviality.

writings.title: Balvenie Portwood 21
writings.summary: Happy International Scotch Day! A look at one of the silkiest smooth whiskies from Balvenie, finished in 30 year old port cask by Master Blender David Stewart.

writings.title: Fuubutsushi
writings.summary: I named the drink "Fuubutsushi" which is Japanese, it means things which remind of a season. A lavender-infused gin cocktail inspired by the lavender fields of Hokkaido.

writings.title: Between the Wires: An interview with Nolan Lawson
writings.summary: An interview with Nolan Lawson, Web Performance PM at Microsoft Edge. He also maintains the popular open source library PouchDB together with others.

writings.title: Autumn in June
writings.summary: Made this autumn drink when the summer evening got a bit chilly. When Sukkah Hill Spirits sent me some samples, I knew I wanted to try their Besamim liqueur, which has the notes of vanilla, clove and cinnamon.

writings.title: Lemon Lavender Gin
writings.summary: Lavender blossom in my back yard and I know it will be all gone when I am back. A simple and pretty cocktail with St George Botanivore Gin, lemon juice, and homemade lavender syrup.

writings.title: Minato Fog
writings.summary: Nikka coffey whisky is known for its sweet spice, mango aroma, perfect for a cold and moody evening. This drink reminds me a lot of the moody mornings in Tokyo.

writings.title: First Word Morning Edition
writings.summary: A riff on the #wehavethelastword campaign featuring St George Nola coffee liqueur, Remy Martin brandy, Fernet Branca, Graham's tawny port, and egg.

writings.title: The Last Word from the Far East
writings.summary: Who says you can't have a cocktail in a bowl? Here is my attempt to add an asian flare to The Last Word.

writings.title: The Tipsy Muse: My Creative Outlet
writings.summary: A little over a year ago, I started a new Instagram account called Tipsy Muse. I created it to document my journey through creating, and photographing countless cocktails.

writings.title: Between the Wires: An interview with Charlie Cheever
writings.summary: An interview with Charlie Cheever, founder of expo.io. Expo's mission is to let web developers build truly native apps that work across both iOS and Android by writing them once in just JavaScript.

writings.title: Between the Wires: An interview with Evan You
writings.summary: An interview with Evan You, the creator of Vue.js, a popular progressive JavaScript framework. Evan works on Vue full time with the funding from the Patreon campaign.

writings.title: Between the Wires: An interview with Irene Ros
writings.summary: An interview with Irene Ros, founding Director of Data Visualization at Bocoup where she has led visualization projects with partners like Google, The Guardian, and The World Economic Forum.

writings.title: Between the Wires: An interview with Guillermo Rauch
writings.summary: An interview with Guillermo Rauch, founder of zeit.co. Zeit's mission is to make cloud deployment simple, global, and real time. Rauch also built socket.io.

writings.title: Designing for Accidental Disconnects
writings.summary: Frequently not having any data connection in even the wealthiest and most developed cities of the world has led us to conclude that the mobile connectivity issue isn't just going to solve itself.

writings.title: Purposeful Design for User Permission
writings.summary: I love mobile design because it's full of constraints. Those constraints force us to think carefully about how and when we present information to the user.

writings.title: Why Chop
writings.summary: As the mother of two small children, and the founding engineering manager of the Chrome Developer Relations team at Google, I've led a busy life. I started Chop Technologies to deliver an Amazon-like experience at local businesses.

writings.title: How We Built Chop
writings.summary: Chop is a service for mobile commerce, designed with the following goals in mind: beautiful and performant UI, fast iteration, easy to theme, robust backend, and support for many platforms.

writings.title: Matcha Pisco
writings.summary: Have been creative recently and made my own version of Matcha Pisco — a cocktail combining matcha and pisco infusion with orange liqueur, jack fruit syrup, and egg white.

writings.title: Celine Fizz
writings.summary: Celine Fizz for the hump day. A refreshing gin fizz with St-Germain, lemon juice, simple syrup, and egg white, topped with club soda.

writings.title: Fast and Fluid Infinite List with React Native
writings.summary: Infinite List, Windowed List, and RecyclerView are not new. Their ideas are rather simple: render only what's visible from the user's viewport, recycle all item view instances with a smart backing data store.
